terminologyProfile.descriptionRule: Use measurable 3D graphics terms. Avoid vague words unless they are paired with concrete geometry/material/shader parameters.

referenceCamera.note: For likeness work, solve the reference camera (forge/stage1_intake/solve_camera_pose.py) so the review render aligns with the photo and the reference can be projected. Confirm by overlay review.

objectClass.notes: Clamshell laptop, lid open ~102 degrees. Two macro bodies (deck, lid) joined by a hinge bar whose pivot lies on the rear edge of the deck. Deck top carries a dense keycap array and a recessed trackpad. Reference is a synthetic render on a flat backdrop, so lighting is clean and the silhouette is unambiguous.

reasoning: Silhouette is simple (two slabs at an angle) but the keycap array is the dominant detail system.
Hierarchy must be 4 deep: root > lid > screen-face, root > deck > keyboard > keycap.
Action readiness is high: the lid hinge is the whole point of the model.
Occlusion is low - two reference views cover front and three-quarter; the underside is unseen and mirrored from the top plate.

specDepthDecision.rationale: The keycap array is a genuine repetition system (rows of varying key widths), and the trackpad/bezel need local material overrides against the body metal. The hinge demands an action-ready pivot.

detailInventory.note: Enumerate every identity-defining small detail before authoring the spec. Each detail must map to a component.localFeatures entry or material.localOverrides entry, never prose only. Use forge/stage1_intake/build_detail_inventory.py to scan zones.

details.description: Front and side edges of the deck carry a visible chamfer that catches a bright rim highlight against the darker top face.
Dense grid of individual keycaps, ~14 columns x 5 rows, each separated by a dark gap; keycap tops are slightly inset from their base footprint.
Row-dependent key widths - modifiers (Tab/Caps/Shift/Enter/Backspace) are wider than letter keys, and the bottom row carries a long spacebar.
Rectangular trackpad set into the palm rest, delimited by a thin dark seam; its surface reads marginally darker and smoother than the surrounding deck.
Darker horizontal bar spanning the deck's rear edge where the lid attaches - reads as a distinct part, not a painted line.
Dark bezel frames the active display area on all four sides, wider at the bottom; the panel sits recessed behind it.
Display panel is self-lit and spills a cool tint onto the keyboard deck below it - an emissive channel, not a bright albedo.
Body metal is satin, not mirror: broad soft speculars along the chamfers with mid roughness across the flats, no sharp reflections.

anatomy.note: Only meaningful when objectClass.primaryDomain is character or hybrid. Set applies=true and fill from forge/stage1_intake/extract_landmarks.py. See grimoire/character/reconstruction.md and grimoire/character/likeness_maximization.md.

definitionOfDone: The rendered model matches the reference silhouette, primary proportions, visible component hierarchy, material response, and most recognizable local features for the selected fidelity tier.

featureGroups.name: Overall silhouette and proportions
qualityCriteria: Bounding shape, dominant curves, negative spaces, and scale relationships are explicitly described.
failureModes: model reads as a generic placeholder instead of the reference object
major proportions are guessed without evidence

featureGroups.name: Primary structure and hierarchy
qualityCriteria: Major parts, joints, seams, contact points, and parent-child relationships are named before code generation.
failureModes: large visible parts are merged into one mesh
component hierarchy is too shallow for the observed complexity

featureGroups.name: Attachment and joint correctness
qualityCriteria: Every visible child appendage, branch, limb, handle, connector, tube, cable, horn, wing, leg, or hinged part has an attachment contract with parent socket, localStart/localEnd, contact type, embed/overlap, and gap tolerance.
failureModes: child part root floats away from the parent
branch/limb/tube is centered in space instead of pivoting from its root
parent-child transform mixes world and local coordinates

featureGroups.name: Surface material response
qualityCriteria: Albedo zones, roughness, normal/bump/displacement intent, cavity dirt, edge wear, and local overrides are specified where visible.
Important materials define independent albedo, roughness, height/normal, and AO responses instead of reusing one texture for unrelated PBR channels.
Surface response is decomposed into macro, meso, and micro frequency bands with scale and amplitude tied to object scale.
failureModes: surface looks like flat plastic
local material variation is missing or not tied to image evidence

featureGroups.name: Reference color, material, and lighting response
qualityCriteria: Material-pass names the reference-derived albedo palette, roughness variation, tactile normal/bump/displacement response, and local masks.
When a source image is available, run reference PBR extraction and require confidence >= 0.7 before treating maps as implementation-ready.
Lighting-pass names key/fill/rim or environment light, exposure, tone mapping, background, and contact shadow behavior.
Neutral, grazing-angle, and reference-matched renders prove that surface relief survives relighting and is not painted into albedo.
failureModes: model has acceptable shape but reads as flat shaded or plastic
colors are a generic average instead of reference-observed local color zones
lighting is evenly ambient and cannot reproduce the source value range

visualDeltaChecks: local feature placement and scale delta
antiShallowSpecRules: Do not proceed to code if qualityContract.qualityBar is unassessed.
Do not proceed to code if the spec only contains a root component for a moderate or complex object.
Do not proceed to code if required featureGroups are not represented by componentTree, materials, or repetitionSystems.
Do not proceed to code if visible local features are described only in prose and not attached to components/materials/evidenceRefs.
Do not proceed past structural-pass if attached child parts lack attachment.parentSocket, localStart, localEnd, embedDepth/overlap, and gapTolerance.
Do not pass material look-dev when albedo is reused as roughness, height, normal, or AO.
Do not pass material look-dev without macro, meso, and micro surface frequency bands for close-up materials.
Do not pass reference-fidelity material look-dev from a source image without usable referencePbr maps or an explicit documented limitation.
Do not patch a spec with extracted PBR maps when extraction confidence is below the target threshold unless the user explicitly accepts lower fidelity.

mustMatch: reference-derived PBR material response at or above 0.7 confidence when source pixels are usable
niceToHave: micro scratches, stains, chips, and dirt masks

visualAcceptance.scoringRule: AI vision must inspect a side-by-side reference/render sheet and score the current pass from 0 to 1. Pixel-diff code may assist diagnostics but cannot approve a pass.

featureReviewPolicy.selectionRule: Choose only the most visually salient, identity-defining, user-prioritized, or high-risk semantic systems. Group repeated parts instead of reviewing every mesh. AI vision scores every selected feature from the same full reference/render pair.

specRefineTriggers: local feature not traceable to viewEvidence
codeRefineTriggers: spec is adequate but generated geometry/material does not match
lighting hides geometry or material response
stopCriteria: target fidelity reached or user accepts current approximation
remaining gaps require new reference images or manual art

screenshotPolicy.minimumEvidence: Each visual pass needs a reference image, rendered screenshot, side-by-side comparison sheet, AI vision score, layer scores, and critique before choosing continue.
screenshotPolicy.reviewPairRule: Compare the same camera/viewpoint whenever possible; do not judge a front reference against a random render angle.
screenshotPolicy.acceptanceAuthority: AI vision review of the comparison sheet. Code-generated pixel similarity is not sufficient evidence.

featureReviewTargets.name: Lid hinge pivot and open angle
featureReviewTargets.criteria: Lid rotates about the deck's rear top edge; no gap or interpenetration at the hinge; open angle matches the reference recline.
failureModes: lid pivots about its own centre
lid floats above the deck

featureReviewTargets.name: Keycap array density and row layout
featureReviewTargets.criteria: Key count, row count and modifier-key widths read like the reference; gaps are even and the spacebar spans the bottom row.
failureModes: uniform key widths
wrong row count
gaps too wide - reads as tiles not keys

featureReviewTargets.name: Deck and lid proportions
featureReviewTargets.criteria: Deck width:depth:thickness and lid height match the reference silhouette; the machine reads thin.
failureModes: deck too thick
lid too tall - reads as a monitor

featureReviewTargets.name: Emissive panel response
featureReviewTargets.criteria: Panel is self-lit through an emissive channel and spills light onto the deck; bezel stays matte-dark.
failureModes: bright albedo instead of emissive
no spill onto the keyboard

featureReviewTargets.name: Chamfer highlight on body edges
featureReviewTargets.criteria: Chamfered edges catch a distinctly brighter satin line than the flats.
failureModes: perfectly sharp edges
uniform roughness everywhere

nextRequiredEvidence: Quality contract has named all required macro feature groups before code generation.
browser render screenshot from your agent's browser/screenshot tool
single side-by-side full reference/render comparison sheet
all critical semantic feature scores at or above their thresholds
self-correction review appended with action=continue before the next pass

referencePbrExtraction.acceptedLimitation: single-image extraction is reference-derived inference, not exact photogrammetry

mustAvoid: plastic-looking smooth bark, stone, cloth, foliage, or aged material
local color/detail described only in prose without material masks
claiming exact PBR recovery when confidence is below the target threshold
reference lighting copied without separating material readability

screenshotReview: Compare albedo palette and local color zones.
Compare cavity dirt, edge wear, stains, moss, scratches, or other local masks.
Compare key/fill/rim structure, exposure, tone mapping, background, and contact shadows.
Capture a neutral-light render to verify material readability without reference lighting.
Capture a grazing-light close-up to expose flat normals, uniform roughness, tiling, and plastic highlights.
Capture a reference-matched render from the same camera framing as the source.

actionReadiness.contract: Every macro/meso component should be generated as a stable named Object3D pivot node with a mesh child, action metadata, optional sockets, collider proxy, and destruction metadata.
requiredComponentFields: attachment for child appendages, connectors, limbs, tubes, handles, legs, horns, wings, branches, or cables
authoringRules: Do not collapse independently movable parts into one mesh.
Put transforms on component pivot groups, not only on raw meshes.
For attached child parts, put the pivot at the semantic root/socket and build visible geometry from localStart to localEnd.
Represent hinge, socket, detachable, and breakable intent even when no animation is implemented yet.
Use simplified collider proxies for runtime physics instead of visual mesh colliders by default.

destructionPolicy.fractureGroupNaming: Use stable semantic names such as body-shell, left-hinge, glass-panel, branch-segment.
destructionPolicy.debrisStrategy: Prefer detachable component groups and a small number of procedural fragments over random mesh explosion.

assumptions: Underside of the deck is never visible in either reference view - mirror the top plate profile and keep it featureless.
Port cutouts on the side walls are not resolvable at this resolution - omit rather than invent.
Screen content is a baked photo texture in the reference; the pilot deliberately substitutes a neutral emissive panel.
Deck underside is never visible in either reference view; it mirrors the top plate profile and stays featureless.
Keycap legends are below the resolvable resolution of the reference and are deliberately omitted rather than invented.
Trim PBR evidence is conditional (0.647 < 0.7 threshold): a flat dark matte surface yields little extractable signal. Trim is authored from direct observation instead and flagged utility.

coordinateFrame.originPolicy: deck footprint centre, resting on Y=0

silhouette.boundingShape: two thin slabs meeting at a rear hinge, lid reclined ~102 degrees from the deck plane
dominantCurves: shallow rounded corners on both slabs
negativeSpaces: wedge of empty space between the open lid and the deck
scaleRelationships: lid height is ~0.93 of deck depth
deck thickness is ~0.028 of deck width - the machine reads as thin
keyboard plate spans ~0.93 of deck width

geometryDescriptor.groupingIntent: container group in code; box is the bounding proxy for spec purposes

dimensions.note: container node - unit scale on purpose; children carry the real sizes

actionProfile.note: staged by the rig (turntable), not self-animated

localFeatures.placement: all four top edges, strongest along the front lip
localFeatures.materialEffect: catches a bright rim highlight against the darker top face

actionProfile.note: the machine rests on this; it never moves relative to root

pivot.note: ON the hinge line - the whole point: rotating about the lid centre would sink it through the deck

actionProfile.note: THE action of this model: rotation.x opens/closes the clamshell

localFeatures.materialEffect: reads darker than the body metal
localFeatures.geometryEffect: distinct cylindrical part, not a painted line

actionProfile.note: visually bridges deck and lid; the pivot lives on lid

localFeatures.geometryEffect: modifier keys wider than letter keys; long bottom spacebar

localFeatures.materialEffect: thin dark AO line, surface slightly smoother than the deck
localFeatures.geometryEffect: inset panel with a shallow seam

actionProfile.note: inset panel, no motion in this pilot

localFeatures.materialEffect: self-lit; spills a cool tint onto the deck below

localFeatures.placement: frames the display on all four sides, wider at the bottom

localFeatures.geometryEffect: panel sits recessed behind the bezel plane

localFeatures.materialEffect: narrow highlight along each cap edge
localFeatures.geometryEffect: top face inset from the base footprint

actionProfile.note: keys are visual only in this pilot; per-key press would need individual pivots

localFeatures.materialEffect: broad soft specular, mid roughness - satin not mirror

actionProfile.note: no independent motion - rides the deck

localFeatures.materialEffect: thin shadow line where the panel drops back

albedo.samplingNotes: sampled from reference crops, not a single averaged colour

wear.notes: reference is a clean product render - no wear present

ao.source: cavity-biased procedural AO along seams and key gaps

localOverrides.region: chamfer bands on deck and lid
localOverrides.note: satin sheen along the chamfer; flats stay rougher
localOverrides.evidence: bright rim visible on the front lip in the front view

materials.qualityNote: PBR extraction plateaus at 0.647 confidence regardless of crop size: a flat dark matte surface carries almost no roughness/normal signal. Marked utility rather than inflating the number — the trim is a supporting surface, not a hero material.

layout.note: widths in key units (1u = a letter key); the layout is authored, not random

jitter.note: a product render has no key jitter; seed kept for future wear variation

acceptance: Silhouette reads correctly without materials.
Quality contract has named all required macro feature groups before code generation.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

buildPasses.goal: Build the component hierarchy implied by the pre-spec complexity assessment.
acceptance: Macro, meso, and repeated structures meet qualityContract.minimumSpecDepth.
Parent-child relations, joints, seams, sockets, and contact points are explicit.
Every attached child appendage/connector has parentSocket, localStart/localEnd, contactType, embedDepth or overlap, and gapTolerance.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

buildPasses.goal: Refine shape, deformation, bevels, tapers, curves, asymmetry, and visible local geometry.
acceptance: Important visible forms are represented in component geometryDescriptor, deformations, localFeatures, or repetitionSystems.
Endpoint-based child parts are rooted at their attachment sockets and do not visibly float away from parents.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

buildPasses.goal: Match material color, roughness, bump, and local variation.
acceptance: Reference-derived albedo palette records dominant, secondary, and accent colors per visible material.
Each important material defines roughness variation and at least one normal/bump/displacement response.
Local material overrides, dirt/wear/stains/moss/chips/scratches or equivalent masks are tied to evidenceRefs.
Thin, transparent, reflective, wet, or fibrous materials document alpha/transmission/clearcoat/metalness/fiber response when relevant.
Generated preview uses procedural albedo/roughness/bump texture or vertex color variation instead of one flat color.
Generated preview uses independent PBR maps at 1024px or higher for the quality-first tier.
If source pixels are available, referencePbr extraction passed at confidence >= 0.7 or the pass is stopped/requesting better references.
Macro, meso, and micro surface frequency bands are visible at the intended review distance without obvious tiling.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

ao.note: concentrates in key gaps, the trackpad seam and the hinge recess

wear.note: clean product render - deliberately no wear

buildPasses.goal: Add procedural surface locality such as normal/bump/displacement, AO, dirt, stains, chips, grain, moss, scratches, and wear.
acceptance: Every required material feature group has local overrides or surfaceDetail tied to evidenceRefs.
A grazing-angle close-up proves that normal/height detail breaks highlights naturally and does not read as smooth plastic.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

buildPasses.goal: Make material and form readable under neutral turntable lighting plus optional reference lighting.
acceptance: lightingFromPhoto identifies key light direction/color/intensity, fill light, rim or environment light, and ambient color.
Exposure, tone mapping, background color/gradient, shadow softness, and contact shadow behavior are specified.
Lighting does not hide geometry/material gaps and screenshots can be compared fairly to the reference.
Neutral, grazing, and reference-matched lighting checks distinguish material errors from lighting errors.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

lights.note: opposite side, lifts the shadowed flank
behind the lid, separates it from the backdrop
three's RoomEnvironment through PMREM - gives the metal something to reflect

shadows.note: grounds the machine on the flat backdrop; without it the laptop floats

buildPasses.goal: Make the model ready for future animation, transformation, physics, or destruction.
acceptance: Macro and movable meso components have stable pivot nodes.
Sockets, collider proxies, and destruction metadata are present for future runtime actions.
AI vision comparison score meets selfCorrectLoop.visualAcceptance.threshold.

buildPasses.goal: Protect runtime performance after visual fidelity is accepted.
acceptance: Triangle count, draw calls, instancing, LOD strategy, and FPS target are documented or verified.
Repeated detail is instanced or simplified where possible without breaking silhouette/material believability.

reviewHistory.summary: Deck proportions and the two-slab silhouette read correctly, but the lid stands vertical instead of reclined and a parasitic connector block sits mid-deck from the lid attachment vector. Below the 0.7 acceptance threshold.

lodPlan.strategy: full component tree and material layers
merge static components and reduce local feature geometry

performanceBudget.optimizationPolicy: Reach accepted visual fidelity first, then optimize without removing reference-critical geometry or surface layers.

lightingFromPhoto: key: upper-front-right point light (~[4,5,4], intensity 240, neutral white) — drives the bright satin line along the deck chamfer and the top edge of the lid.
fill: opposite-side point light (~[-5,3.5,-2], intensity 160, neutral white) — lifts the shadowed flank so the body reads as satin metal rather than flat grey.
rim: low-intensity cool light behind the lid (~[0,2,-4], intensity 90, #c8d4ff) — separates the lid silhouette from the flat backdrop.
environment: three RoomEnvironment through PMREM at 0.65 — without it the metal renders near-black because there is nothing to reflect.
practical: the display panel is itself an emissive source and spills a cool tint onto the keyboard deck; it is part of the lighting, not just a bright surface.
exposure and tone mapping: ACES filmic tone mapping, no adaptive exposure — the reference is a clean product render with unclipped highlights on the chamfers.
contact/ground shadow: soft contact shadow under the deck footprint grounds the machine on the backdrop - without it the model reads as floating.
proceduralStrategy: Create stable pivot groups, sockets, collider proxies, and destruction metadata before visual polish.
Refine forms with bevels, tapers, bends, and procedural noise.
Run reference PBR extraction for important source-image materials and stop when confidence is below the target threshold.
Add material variation before adding expensive micro-geometry.
animationAnchors: root pivot node supports whole-object translation, rotation, scale, and visibility changes
component pivot groups support later local transforms without rebuilding geometry
destructionAnchors: actionProfile.destruction.fractureGroup marks detachable or breakable component sets
component seams and sockets define plausible break points instead of random explosions
